reason: Cross-package thinking guides are relevant because publishing spans database, backend routes, external API integration, shared types, and frontend workflow.
Publishing data flows across Prisma models, services, routes, shared contracts, and UI state.
The implementation should reuse existing novel, chapter, ownership, API, query key, and task/status patterns before adding helpers.
Backend publishing routes, services, external API client, migrations, and ownership checks are central to the task.
New backend modules should follow project service and route organization.
Publishing requires new persisted models and migrations for PostgreSQL and SQLite without destructive database commands.
External dispatch errors and relogin requirements need consistent API error handling.
Backend changes need to follow project quality constraints and avoid brittle fallback logic.
Publishing platform work must follow the server-owned dispatch, timer format, grouping, status mapping, QR sanitization, and ownership contracts.
The publishing workspace needs new UI inside the client novel workflow.
New publishing components should match existing component patterns.
Publishing API calls and server state should follow existing hook/data-fetching conventions.
Publishing plan editing and status refresh require clear local vs server state boundaries.
Publishing forms, API responses, and status enums need strong TypeScript contracts.
Frontend implementation should preserve responsive layout, accessible copy, and project quality bar.
Shared publishing types may be added or exported for client/server contracts.
Shared contracts should keep status and schedule fields type-safe.
External Fanqie dispatch API behavior, status enums, relogin flow, and timer format drive implementation.
